devdoc.details: Each state-changing function:      1. Validates the caller's role      2. Validates the batch is in the correct preceding state      3. Updates the batch status via MintContract      4. Appends a CustodyRecord to the batch history      5. Emits an event

params.mintContractAddress: The deployed address of the MintContract.

params.tokenId: The batch token being received. TODO: Enforce that msg.sender == currentCustodian[tokenId].

release(uint256,address).details: Preconditions:      - Batch must exist and have status Produced      - msg.sender must be the original manufacturer of the batch      - distributor must be an authorised distributor TODO: Validate that distributor address is authorised before proceeding. TODO: Validate that msg.sender matches batch.manufacturer (not just any manufacturer).

params.distributor: The distributor address receiving the batch.

params.notes: Optional shipment notes (e.g. carrier ID, temperature log reference). TODO: Enforce that msg.sender == currentCustodian[tokenId]. TODO: Consider allowing multi-hop shipments (InTransit → InTransit).
params.recipient: The address of the pharmacy or hospital receiving the batch.

getCustodyHistory(uint256).notice: Returns the full custody history array for a batch.

mintContract().notice: Reference to the MintContract for batch lookups and status updates

receipt(uint256).notice: Step 3 — Pharmacy or hospital confirms receipt of a batch.

release(uint256,address).notice: Step 1 — Manufacturer releases a produced batch to a distributor.

shipment(uint256,address,string).notice: Step 2 — Distributor records a shipment event.

userdoc.notice: Manages the internal supply chain pipeline for pharmaceutical batches.         Handles custody transfers: Manufacturer → Distributor → Pharmacy/Hospital. Permitted actions per role:   Manufacturer  → release()   Distributor   → shipment()   Pharmacy      → receipt()

devdoc.details: This is a simplified token registry (not a full ERC-721).      Each batch gets a unique tokenId incremented from 1.      The ControlContract reads from this contract to validate batches.

params.tokenId: The batch token to look up.

mintBatch(string,string,uint256).details: TODO: Add validation:      - expiryDate must be in the future      - medicineName and batchNumber must not be empty strings      - Consider emitting a QR-code-friendly hash of the tokenId

params.expiryDate: Unix timestamp of the batch expiry date.

returns.tokenId: The ID assigned to the newly minted batch.

updateStatus(uint256,uint8).details: TODO: Restrict this so only the ControlContract address can call it.      One approach: store the ControlContract address in state and add a modifier.

devdoc.title: MintContract

documentation.text: @notice Represents the current stage of a batch in the supply chain
@notice Core data stored for each pharmaceutical batch
@notice A single entry in the custody history of a batch

@title ControlContract
 @notice Manages the internal supply chain pipeline for pharmaceutical batches.
         Handles custody transfers: Manufacturer → Distributor → Pharmacy/Hospital.
 Permitted actions per role:
   Manufacturer  → release()
   Distributor   → shipment()
   Pharmacy      → receipt()
 @dev Each state-changing function:
      1. Validates the caller's role
      2. Validates the batch is in the correct preceding state
      3. Updates the batch status via MintContract
      4. Appends a CustodyRecord to the batch history
      5. Emits an event
@notice Reference to the MintContract for batch lookups and status updates
@notice Contract owner — manages role assignments
@notice Role mappings
@notice Maps tokenId → ordered list of custody records
@notice Maps tokenId → current custodian address
